title: RV8GR fetch-cycle trace
purpose: Package the first full fetch/execute trace so T0 control fetch, T1 operand fetch, T2 LI execution, PC motion, and bus ownership stay testable outside the full CPU.

chips.role: ROM bytes for the golden trace

wiring.rule: U34 drives immediate only during T2 immediate execution
U7 bridges DBUS to IBUS during T0/T1 fetch

behavior.t0: ROM byte at PC drives DBUS, U7 drives IBUS, U5 captures IRH, and PC increments.
behavior.t1: ROM byte at PC drives DBUS, U7 drives IBUS, U6 captures IRL, and PC increments.
behavior.t2_li: U34 drives IRL onto IBUS, LI writes AC, Z updates, and PC holds.
behavior.bus_policy: Every trace row has at most one active IBUS driver and at most one active DBUS driver.

timing.pc_increment: PC increments only on T0 and T1; T2 holds unless branch/jump loads PC.
timing.frequency_boundary: Functional trace proof only; physical timing is owned by timing_margins.json.

guarantees: basic fetch matches tb_rv8gr_tasks.v for LI $42
PC increments on T0/T1 and holds on LI T2
bus-driver roles match the documented trace
no IBUS or DBUS contention is accepted

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
